subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Interpret and balance complex chemical equations using the Law of Conservation of Mass
Classify and predict the products of various chemical reactions
Explain the mechanism of displacement and double displacement reactions
Differentiate between exothermic and endothermic reactions with energy profiles
Analyze redox reactions in terms of gain/loss of oxygen and hydrogen
Apply knowledge of corrosion and rancidity to real-world preservation techniques

lesson_plan.title: Chemical Equations and Balancing
topics_covered: Significance of a balanced chemical equation
Writing state symbols and reaction conditions
student_activities: Balance a set of 10 equations of increasing difficulty
Identify mistakes in purposely 'wrong' balanced equations

lesson_plan.title: Combination and Decomposition Reactions
topics_covered: Thermal decomposition of Ferrous Sulphate and Lead Nitrate
teaching_strategy: Demonstration of heating Lead Nitrate (brown fumes)
Comparison of energy absorption vs. release
Video on Photolytic decomposition of Silver Chloride
student_activities: Draw a detailed labeled diagram of water electrolysis
Record observations of color changes in Ferrous Sulphate crystals
assessment: Short answer questions on gas identification

lesson_plan.title: Displacement Reactions
topics_covered: Reaction of Zinc/Iron with Copper Sulphate
teaching_strategy: Case studies: Why does color change occur?
student_activities: Perform the Iron nail experiment and record time-based changes
Predict outcomes of reactions based on the reactivity series

lesson_plan.title: Double Displacement and Precipitation
topics_covered: Reaction between Barium Chloride and Sodium Sulphate
teaching_strategy: Live demonstration of white precipitate formation
student_activities: Write the chemical formulas for precipitates formed in given reactions
assessment: Identifying the 'Precipitate' from a list of products

lesson_plan.title: Oxidation and Reduction (Redox)
teaching_strategy: Step-by-step analysis of the $CuO + H_{2}$ reaction
student_activities: Labeling Redox components in 5 specific equations

lesson_plan.title: Effects of Oxidation in Daily Life
topics_covered: Corrosion of metals (Rusting, Black coating on Silver, Green on Copper)
teaching_strategy: Analyzing food packaging labels for antioxidants
Group debate: Economic impact of corrosion
student_activities: Investigate household items for signs of oxidation
Create a comparison table for prevention methods

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Board-Pattern Assessment
topics_covered: Consolidated review of all 5 reaction types
student_activities: Complete a comprehensive chapter summary map
assessment: Analysis of common errors in balancing/formula writing

teaching_aids: Interactive Digital Simulator for balancing equations
teacher_tip: At this level, students often struggle with the 'Ion Exchange' concept in Double Displacement. Use colored chalk or markers to represent different ions and physically 'swap' them on the board to make the concept concrete.
